batch_description: ACH (Analysis of Competing Hypotheses) stress-test batch built from real, public, source-contained documents. Each fixture pairs a verbatim source document with an ach_payload.json that names competing hypotheses, diagnostic evidence rows anchored to exact source phrases, and an expected_read (best hypothesis + grounded rationale + sensitivity expectation). The batch is designed to test whether an ACH routine measures real evidentiary sensitivity rather than always reporting zero: it contains a deliberate spread of sensitivities, from a balanced single-row-flip fixture to a triple-anchored robust-winner fixture. Each fixture also carries 15 source-contained QA pairs (60 total).

sensitivity_discrimination_pair.note: These two fixtures bracket the test. The NTSB report flips its winning hypothesis when a single evidence row (empty tanks, no leak) is removed; the SEC 8-K does not flip under any single-row removal because its winner is anchored by three independent passages. An ACH engine that reports the same sensitivity for both, or that cannot tell a flip case from a no-flip case, is not measuring sensitivity.

fixtures.document_type: NTSB Aviation Investigation Final Report (general-aviation accident, probable cause weighed against alternatives)
fixtures.why_good_ach: Three genuinely competing single-cause explanations (fuel exhaustion, carburetor icing, throttle/mechanical), each with affirmative and undercutting evidence in the same report. THE designated balanced fixture: removing the empty-tanks/no-leak finding plausibly flips the winner from fuel exhaustion to carburetor icing.

fixtures.target_category: FDA warning letter: process / documentation / quality-system / vendor-supplier responsibility framings
fixtures.document_type: FDA CGMP warning letter to a distributor/product-owner whose contract manufacturer was the source of adulteration
fixtures.why_good_ach: All four responsibility framings the brief names are present in the source, and the document explicitly forecloses two (vendor-only and documentation/COA) while subsuming a third (specific decisions) into the cited quality-system violation. Robust-winner counterpart that tests whether the engine correctly reports NO single-row flip.

fixtures.target_category: SEC 8-K / financial disclosure: competing explanations for a restatement / control weakness
fixtures.document_type: Form 8-K Item 4.02 (non-reliance) restating segment-information footnotes, tied to a previously identified material weakness
fixtures.why_good_ach: Competing reads (material-weakness vs contained no-earnings-impact reclassification vs SEC-staff-driven change) all have textual support, but the winning material-weakness reading is anchored by three independent passages, so no single-row removal flips it. The low-sensitivity control in the discrimination pair.

fixtures.target_category: Legal/regulatory order with contested chronology or actor responsibility
fixtures.document_type: SEC settled administrative cease-and-desist order making findings; locates responsibility across disclosure controls, cybersecurity personnel, and disclosure drafters
fixtures.why_good_ach: Three competing loci of responsibility for misleading disclosures, with the order framing personnel failures as downstream of a controls-design gap. Several evidence rows are double-edged. Winner is robust to single-row removal but flips under a plausible two-row removal (medium), plus a forensically-gapped chronology for date/sequence reasoning.

ach_payload_contract.note: Every text_anchor is an exact or near-exact phrase from the corresponding source.md, locatable via the bracketed section coordinates. Every expected_read.rationale is grounded only in source.md.

discipline_notes: All four sources are real, public documents (NTSB CAROL, FDA.gov, SEC EDGAR, SEC.gov litigation). URLs are preserved in each fixture's source_url.txt.

Source-contained: no QA answer and no ACH read requires outside legal translation, agency background, acronym expansion, or 'everyone knows' domain facts unless that fact is also stated in source.md. Where a regulation, statute section, or technical term is load-bearing, the source document itself states what it means.

Exactly one fixture (ntsb_engine_power_001) is balanced with a single-row winner flip; the SEC fixture is the deliberate low-sensitivity control; together they form the sensitivity discrimination pair.
No Prethinker internals (predicates, helpers, carriers, compile-surface terms) are referenced anywhere in the batch; fixture_notes.md describes only properties of the source documents.

Government-authored sources (NTSB, FDA, SEC order) are public domain; the SEC EDGAR 8-K is a public record. Redaction markers and forensic-gap caveats in the originals are preserved or faithfully summarized.
fixture_files_per_folder: source.md — verbatim source text with bracketed section coordinates
source_url.txt — canonical public URL of the source document
source_original.txt — verbatim text capture with provenance header
metadata.json — fixture-level metadata (category, counts, expected best hypothesis, sensitivity, pivotal row)
fixture_notes.md — narrative on why the document is a good ACH test, how it adjudicates, and why the sensitivity level is what it is
qa.md — 15 source-contained questions with answers and coordinates
oracle.jsonl — one JSON record per question (id, question, answer, reference_answer, category, source_coords, ach_link, pressure_tags, source_contained)
ach_payload.json — the ACH payload (hypotheses, evidence_rows, expected_read)
